rulings.Case Title: MYHOA NGUYEN VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiff?S Proofs Of Service Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 418.10
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, March 24, 2025 Line 2. DEFENDANTs LLOYD DAMON, MATTHEW OERTLI, SHAGUN ARORA, FEIFEI XUE, NICOLE APPELLE, MATTHEW ALDRICH, MIGUEL CEREJO, UCSF HEALTH ACO, LLC, UCSF HEALTH COMMUNITY HOSPITALS, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA,and UCSF MEDICAL FOUNDATION's Motion To Quash Plaintiff?S Proofs Of Service Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 418.10. Eleven specially appearing individuals and entities' motion to quash plaintiff's proofs of service pursuant to CCP 418.10 is denied in its entirety. This motion, assertedly brought "out of an abundance of caution to preclude improper entry of default judgments against" the moving parties, is itself improper. The title of the motion invokes CCP 418.10 as the authority for the motion yet that code section says nothing about a motion to quash proofs of service nor is the court aware of any authority that provides for such relief. If and when a party believes that he, she or it was served with a summons impermissibly, then a section 418.10 motion may be appropriate, but none of the moving parties appear to contend that they have been served with a summons. Moreover, since the moving parties are appearing specially and a special appearance is confined to challenging the court's jurisdiction, the moving parties may not raise any issue about the effect of the request for dismissal filed by the plaintiff on July 10, 2023, at least without their appearances being treated as general appearances acceding to the court's jurisdiction. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE R. CALLES ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, March 24, 2025 Line 10. PLAINTIFFs JOSE CALLES,and JOSE CASTILLO PEREZ' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents. Plaintiffs' motion to compel the deposition of the defendant's person most knowledgeable and production of documents is granted. Defendant must produce the PMK and all-nonprivileged responsive documents by April 7, 2025. If any documents are withheld on the grounds of privilege, the withheld documents must be identified in a privilege log. The discovery sought by plaintiffs is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and the defendant's boilerplate objections (e.g., vague, overbroad) lack merit. Plaintiff's "other vehicle" requests regarding the same alleged defects is proper. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge].) Defendant does not present any competent evidence showing that the requests are unduly burdensome. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LORI REESE-BROWN VS. JOHN E COWAN
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, March 24, 2025 Line 8. 1 - DEFENDANT JOHN COWAN DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Off calendar in light of the ruling on plaintiff Lori Reese-Brown's motion to stay proceedings, compel mediation/arbitration, and strike defendant's demurrer as an abuse of process. The parties are required to mediate their disputes and, if unsuccessful in reaching a settlement through mediation, are required to arbitrate their disputes. This case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)
